essence: Integrates automation, robotics, and CNC systems to create efficient, high‑precision manufacturing workflows.

identity.title: CNC Automation Engineer
identity.summary: Designs and implements automated CNC systems, integrates robotics and sensors, improves workflow efficiency, and ensures reliable, high‑precision production across advanced manufacturing environments.

salary.notes: Engineers working in aerospace, robotics, or fully automated smart‑factory environments can exceed £100k-£140k+.

qualifications: Experience in CNC machining, manufacturing engineering, or process engineering
Knowledge of robotics, PLCs, sensors, and automation systems
Ability to integrate CNC machines with automated workflows
Understanding of lean manufacturing, quality systems, and industrial controls

lifestyle.description: CNC automation engineers work in advanced manufacturing, aerospace, automotive, robotics, and smart‑factory environments. They integrate CNC machines with automation systems, reduce manual handling, improve throughput, and enhance precision. The role suits individuals who enjoy systems engineering, robotics, and high‑tech manufacturing.

habits: Designs and integrates CNC automation systems
Implements robotics, sensors, and automated workflows
Optimises cycle times, material handling, and machine utilisation
Collaborates with engineers, machinists, and automation teams
Ensures reliability, safety, and quality in automated processes
